rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LAWRENCE LEVENE VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Bruce Tassi'S Deposition And To Hold Him In Contempt For Failure To Appear
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendants Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. and Volvo Cars of North America, LLC¿s motion to compel Bruce Tassi¿s deposition and to hold him in contempt for failure to appear is granted. No opposition filed. Mr. Tassi is compelled to comply with the deposition subpoena served on him on May 1, 2012 on the following terms and conditions: (1) He shall appear for his deposition on August 13, 2012, at 10:00am; (2) The place of the deposition shall be Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP, 44 Montgomery Street, Suite 400, San Francisco, CA 94104; (3) The deposition will occur pursuant to defendants¿ deposition subpoena; and (4) He shall produce all documents and things in his possession, custody or control that are responsive to the requests for documents contained in the subpoena. Mr. Tassi is held in contempt of court pursuant to C.C.P. sections 1209(a)(10), 2023.010(d) and 2023.030(e). A hearing is required unless counsel for defendants notifies Mr. Tassi of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
